headline: A US government export-control directive forced Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 within days of launch.
body: The quarter's dominant story is government intervention in frontier model deployment: Anthropic was ordered to shut down Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers, with sources citing export-control directives, jailbreak vulnerability, and extension of the ban into European jurisdictions. In parallel, an inference-speed race broke out, with Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma and Xiaomi's MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed both clearing 1,000 tokens per second through diffusion-based generation, FP4 quantization, speculative decoding, and persistent GPU kernel runtimes. On the tooling side, Model Context Protocol and JSON-RPC entered as new technique entrants alongside structured actions, schema context injection, and query validation, signaling rotation toward agent integration plumbing. Established techniques including error handling, AI engineering, AI coding assistants, prompt caching, and cache hit rate all registered as lagging.
